title: DailyMed Ozempic / semaglutide label
supports_claim: Semaglutide common adverse reactions include nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal pain and constipation; semaglutide delays gastric emptying and may affect absorption of oral medications.
does_not_support: Does not prove IV/IM support improves GLP-1 efficacy or weight loss.
evidence_directness: direct for GLP-1 GI/oral absorption axis; indirect for wellness support

title: DailyMed Wegovy / semaglutide label
supports_claim: In people with kidney problems, diarrhea, nausea and vomiting may cause dehydration and worsen kidney problems; hydration precautions are relevant.
does_not_support: Does not mean routine IV hydration is indicated for every patient.
evidence_directness: direct for dehydration/renal-risk gate; indirect for hydration support

title: DailyMed Mounjaro / tirzepatide label
supports_claim: Tirzepatide delays gastric emptying and may affect oral medication absorption; hypoglycemia risk increases with insulin/secretagogues.
does_not_support: Does not prove IV support improves GLP/GIP outcomes.
evidence_directness: direct for GIP/GLP gastric emptying and hypoglycemia-risk context

title: DailyMed Zepbound / tirzepatide label
supports_claim: Tirzepatide delays gastric emptying and can affect absorption of concomitant oral medications; oral hormonal contraceptive efficacy context is specifically addressed in labeling.
does_not_support: Does not support IV treatment of drug absorption issues.
evidence_directness: direct for oral absorption/contraceptive review gate

title: NIH ODS Vitamin B12 Fact Sheet for Health Professionals
supports_claim: B12 is relevant to neurologic/hematologic function; metformin/PPI use, malabsorption, GI surgery and restricted diets are deficiency-risk modifiers.
does_not_support: Does not prove B12 is a guaranteed energy booster in nondeficient clients.
evidence_directness: direct for B12 biology/risk; indirect for wellness energy support

title: NIH ODS Biotin Fact Sheet for Health Professionals
supports_claim: Biotin is involved in carboxylase enzyme function and marketed for hair/skin/nails; evidence outside deficiency is limited; lab-test interference is important.
does_not_support: Does not prove hair regrowth or GLP support outcome in nondeficient clients.
evidence_directness: direct for nutrient biology and lab interference; limited for aesthetic outcomes

title: FDA: Biotin Interference with Troponin Lab Tests
supports_claim: Biotin can interfere with certain lab tests including troponin and may cause falsely low results.
does_not_support: Does not prohibit all biotin wellness use.

title: NIH ODS Magnesium Fact Sheet for Health Professionals
supports_claim: Magnesium is involved in energy production and neuromuscular function; toxicity risk increases with impaired renal function.
does_not_support: Does not prove magnesium IV improves GLP outcomes.
evidence_directness: direct for nutrient biology and safety gate

title: MSD/Merck Manual: IV vitamin therapy / Myers' cocktail
supports_claim: IV vitamin therapy is used in wellness settings; broad benefits are not confirmed; kidney disease, hypertension, heart conditions, pregnancy/breastfeeding and medication interactions are caution contexts.
does_not_support: Does not validate GLP-specific IV efficacy.
evidence_directness: direct for evidence-limit and safety posture

title: NIH ODS Vitamin C Fact Sheet for Health Professionals
supports_claim: Vitamin C has collagen/antioxidant biology; high intake has renal/oxalate considerations.
does_not_support: Does not prove GLP-related hair/skin support or weight-loss benefit.
evidence_directness: direct for nutrient biology/safety; indirect for wellness support
